title: Form W-4: Employee's Withholding Certificate
content: Form W-4 is completed by employees and submitted to their employer so that the employer can withhold the correct federal income tax from the employee's pay. The form accounts for filing status, multiple jobs, dependents, other income, and deductions. Employees should submit a new W-4 when their personal or financial situation changes, such as marriage, divorce, or the birth of a child. The 2020 redesign eliminated withholding allowances and replaced them with a more straightforward approach based on expected annual income and deductions.
